resources.string: Detects YouTube Shorts, TikTok short-video feed, Instagram Reels, and Facebook Reels locally when protection is enabled and closes the blocking screen after you leave them.
Parent PIN is required to allow short videos or change protection.
Optional parent-controlled Device Admin protection that makes uninstall harder while active.
Optional tamper protection makes Shorts Blocker Kids harder to uninstall while active. It does not replace Accessibility protection and does not block Settings or perform hidden system actions.
Tamper protection is active. Disabling it can allow Shorts Blocker Kids to be uninstalled or protection to be changed without the parent PIN.

Shorts Blocker Kids uses Android Accessibility Service to detect when protected short-video surfaces are open and show a blocking overlay. The protected surfaces are YouTube Shorts, TikTok short-video feed, Instagram Reels, and Facebook Reels. TikTok regional package com.ss.android.ugc.trill and Facebook Lite are not supported. YouTube Shorts has prior real-device smoke evidence. TikTok, Instagram Reels, and Facebook Reels have detector and unit coverage, but still require real-device QA before they are production-verified.

When short videos are blocked, the parent PIN can temporarily allow access. Without the parent PIN, the blocking overlay stays active while a protected short-video surface is open.

The app does not collect or send child data, supported app activity, watch history, video titles, URLs, account names, comments, messages, screen recordings, audio, location, contacts, browsing history, or raw Accessibility tree dumps. There is no account system, analytics, or ads. Backend behavior is limited to Google Play billing verification. Rules and PIN protection stay locally on this phone.

Blocking works when Protection is ON and Android Accessibility Service is active. If that permission is turned off or the app is removed, blocking stops.
Shorts Blocker Kids works locally on this phone. It does not require an account. Production subscription verification may use a billing backend limited to Google Play entitlement status.

The app uses Android Accessibility Service to detect supported short-video surfaces and show a blocking overlay when protection is enabled. The protected surfaces are YouTube Shorts, TikTok short-video feed, Instagram Reels, and Facebook Reels. TikTok regional package com.ss.android.ugc.trill and Facebook Lite are not supported.

Optional Device Admin tamper protection can make Shorts Blocker Kids harder to uninstall while it is active. It does not replace Accessibility Service, block Android Settings, wipe the device, or perform hidden system actions.
Protection settings and parent PIN hash metadata stay on this phone. The parent PIN is not stored as plain text.

The app does not collect or send child data, supported app activity, watch history, video titles, URLs, account names, comments, messages, screen recordings, audio, location, contacts, browsing history, or raw Accessibility tree dumps.

Subscriptions are handled through Google Play Billing. Payment data is processed by Google Play under Google Play terms. This app stores local subscription entitlement status. Backend verification, when enabled, uses only billing technical data and stores hashed purchase tokens.
Blocking works when Protection is ON and Android Accessibility Service is active. If that permission is turned off or the app is removed after tamper protection is disabled, blocking stops.

Device Admin tamper protection is optional. It makes Shorts Blocker Kids harder to uninstall while it is active, so a child cannot remove protection as easily.

It does not replace Android Accessibility Service. Blocking still works only when Accessibility protection is enabled. This app will not block Android Settings, change hidden system settings, wipe the device, lock the screen, reset passwords, or manage other apps.

If someone tries to disable tamper protection, Android will show a warning before allowing the Device Admin status to be turned off.
Tamper protection is optional and currently inactive
Blocks supported short-video surfaces on a child’s phone.\nNo account. No server. Parent PIN only.
Choose which supported short-video surfaces this parent PIN should protect on this phone.

plurals.name: pin_entry_lockout_minutes
plurals.item: PIN locked. Try again in %1$d min.
PIN locked. Try again in %1$d min.

resources.string: Allow protected short-video apps on this phone for:

1. Tap Open Settings.
2. Find Shorts Blocker Kids.
3. Turn the service on.
4. Return to this app.

Shorts Blocker Kids works when Protection is ON and Android Accessibility Service is active for YouTube Shorts, TikTok short-video feed, Instagram Reels, and Facebook Reels. Optional Device Admin tamper protection can make uninstall harder while active. If Accessibility permission is turned off, blocking stops.
Protection inactive. Turn Protection ON to block short videos.
Protection inactive. Enable at least one protected app.
Free test has not started. Enable protection permission to start the 20-day test.
Protection inactive. Complete setup to block short videos.
supported by code; needs real-device QA
snapshot requested; open YouTube to capture next event
overlay requested; open YouTube to consume next event
